name: Planet Labs PBC

profile.description: Planet Labs operates the largest fleet of Earth-observation satellites, imaging the entire planet daily at up to 3.5m resolution with over 200 satellites. The company provides geospatial data and AI-powered analytics for defense, intelligence, agriculture, and environmental monitoring applications.

innovation.label: STRONG
innovation.analysis: Track Record (3/3): Planet achieved record FY2026 revenue of $307.7M (+26% YoY) with Q4 at $86.8M (+41%), beating estimates by 11.6% [1]. The company reached first-ever non-GAAP profitability (adjusted EBITDA of $15.5M) and free cash flow of $52.9M versus -$64M prior year. Defense & intelligence revenue grew 50%+ YoY. This represents a fundamental transformation from loss-making to profitable operations. AI Deployment (2/2): Planet's 4M daily satellite images are processed with AI for change detection, crop monitoring, damage assessment, and environmental tracking [2]. The petabyte-scale archive serves as 'ground truth' for physical AI, attracting partnerships from Google and NVIDIA. AI is core to the company's analytics layer. Market Share (1/2): Planet operates the largest commercial Earth-observation constellation (200+ satellites), providing daily imaging cadence unmatched by competitors [2]. However, Maxar (now part of Advent International) and Airbus Defence & Space compete with higher-resolution imagery. Pipeline (1/2): $900M+ backlog (79% YoY growth), $240M Germany contract, 9-figure Sweden deal, and FY2027 guidance of $415-440M revenue provide strong visibility [1]. R&D Intensity (1/1): Planet maintains a fleet of 200+ satellites requiring continuous technology development in miniaturized optics, on-board processing, and data analytics.

inspiration.label: STRONG
inspiration.analysis: Mission Floor (3/4): Planet's mission to 'image the Earth every day and make change visible, accessible, and actionable' is specific, thoughtful, and validated by a decade of execution [2]. As a Public Benefit Corporation, the board has a statutory duty to balance stakeholder interests. CEO Will Marshall's NASA background lends authenticity to the environmental monitoring mission. Community Impact (2/2): Planet provides free satellite data to 64 tropical countries through the NICFI program (funded by Norway) to combat deforestation [2]. The company maps solar and wind facilities globally with Microsoft and provides disaster response imagery for humanitarian purposes. Employee Retention (1/2): Founded by ex-NASA scientists in a garage, Planet retains a mission-driven culture. 945 employees include rocket scientists, AI engineers, and researchers drawn by the company's planetary monitoring vision [2]. Insider Buying (1/2): CEO Marshall and co-founders maintain significant equity stakes, having built the company from founding through IPO.

infrastructure.label: DEVELOPING
infrastructure.analysis: Planet's financials show dramatic improvement but still carry legacy challenges. Revenue grew 41.1% YoY in Q4 to $86.8M [1]. D/E of 245% is very elevated. ROE is deeply negative at -78.4% (legacy losses). Profit margin is -80.2% (GAAP, though adjusted EBITDA turned positive). Forward P/E is meaningless (negative). The $10.9B market cap against $308M revenue trades at 35x sales. FCF flipped to +$52.9M, a major inflection point. 5yr revenue CAGR of approximately 25% is strong. Liquidity: $31.83 x 13.2M = $420M daily, excellent [6].

ai_resilience.label: LOW RISK
ai_resilience.analysis: AI Risk Protection (8/9): Planet's physical satellite constellation creating daily global imagery cannot be replicated by AI — you need physical hardware in orbit to capture data [1][2]. AI actually INCREASES demand for Planet's data: AI models need 'ground truth' training data, and Planet's petabyte-scale archive is uniquely comprehensive. Partnerships with Google and NVIDIA validate this. The 200+ satellite fleet creates a multi-year, multi-billion-dollar barrier to entry. AI Opportunity (1/1): Planet generates AI-related revenue through AI-powered analytics products, defense AI applications, and data licensing to AI companies training physical-world models [1][2].

infinite_game.summary: Planet Labs exemplifies infinite game thinking through its decade-long commitment to daily Earth observation, its Public Benefit Corporation structure, and its mission-driven culture founded by ex-NASA scientists. The company accepted years of losses to build a satellite constellation that could not have been created incrementally. However, commercial execution only recently reached viability.

just_cause.analysis: Planet's cause of imaging the Earth every day to make change visible and actionable is deeply articulated and validated by a decade of consistent execution [2]. The PBC structure legally enshrines this purpose. The NICFI program providing free satellite data to combat tropical deforestation demonstrates the cause extends beyond profit. CEO Marshall's 'if you can see it, you can change it' philosophy drives strategic decisions. This is one of the stronger Just Causes in the screening universe (3 pts).

trusting_teams.analysis: Planet was founded by NASA scientists in a garage and has maintained a mission-driven culture through IPO and growth to 945 employees [2]. The team includes rocket scientists, AI engineers, and researchers drawn by the vision. However, years of losses and operational challenges have tested team resilience (1 pt).

worthy_rivals.analysis: Planet acknowledges the competitive landscape from Maxar, Airbus, and emerging smallsat companies but competitive language focuses on Planet's unique daily cadence advantage. No explicit evidence of citing rivals' strengths as motivation for improvement (0 pts).

existential_flexibility.analysis: Planet has evolved from data provider to analytics platform, and now offers 'Satellite-as-a-Service' for nations wanting their own constellations [1]. The pivot from pure imagery to AI-powered analytics and defense applications represents meaningful business model evolution. However, the core satellite data business remains central (1 pt).

courage_to_lead.analysis: Planet invested hundreds of millions in building the world's largest commercial satellite constellation before proving commercial viability — accepting years of losses to build irreplaceable infrastructure [2]. The decision to operate as a PBC with dual stakeholder obligations demonstrates principled long-term thinking (1 pt).

investment_thesis: Planet Labs is at a pivotal inflection point — FY2026 marked first-ever profitability (adjusted EBITDA $15.5M), free cash flow of $52.9M (vs -$64M prior year), and record revenue of $307.7M [1]. The $900M+ backlog (79% YoY growth) provides exceptional revenue visibility, and FY2027 guidance of $415-440M implies 35-43% growth. Defense & intelligence revenue growing 50%+ reflects the geopolitical tailwind for satellite surveillance. Planet's petabyte-scale archive is becoming essential infrastructure for physical AI, attracting partnerships with Google and NVIDIA [2]. The PBC structure and environmental mission provide ESG appeal. CEO Marshall's NASA background and decade-long commitment validate the infinite game thesis.
thesis_continued: The bear case centers on: (1) GAAP profitability remains negative with -80% profit margin, and the stock trades at 35x sales against $308M revenue [1]; (2) D/E of 245% reflects significant leverage from satellite constellation financing; (3) government contract concentration creates revenue volatility — the NICFI Norway contract loss dragged civil government revenue, and defense contracts can be cancelled or delayed; (4) commercial sector revenue was modestly down YoY, suggesting the civilian market is not yet scaling; (5) the FY2027 EBITDA forecast of up to $10M fell short of analyst estimates despite strong revenue guidance, suggesting margin expansion will be slow.
key_catalysts: $900M+ backlog providing 3-year revenue visibility with defense and intelligence contracts accelerating
AI-powered analytics becoming essential training data source for physical AI models — Google and NVIDIA partnerships
FY2027 revenue guidance of $415-440M implies 35-43% growth with continued profitability improvement
key_risks: 35x price-to-sales valuation leaves no margin for execution missteps on growth targets
Government contract concentration creates revenue volatility — NICFI loss impacted civil government segment
D/E of 245% and GAAP losses of -80% profit margin indicate financial fragility despite improving trajectory
